complianceScan.title: HR Compliance Scan
complianceScan.desc: Find out if your company is exposed to MOM regulatory risk. Answer 10 quick questions and receive an instant compliance score, risk rating, and recommended corrective actions — at no cost.

receiveItems.title: Compliance Score
receiveItems.desc: An instant percentage score across 10 key HR compliance areas.

receiveItems.title: Risk Level Rating
receiveItems.desc: Categorised as Low, Medium, High, or Critical risk with a plain-English explanation.

receiveItems.title: Action Checklist
receiveItems.desc: Specific corrective steps tailored to the gaps identified in your answers.

complianceScan.ctaTitle: Ready to check your compliance status?

companyDetails.heading: Your Details
companyDetails.subheading: We'll use this to personalise your compliance report.

intro.aboutSuffix: against Singapore's key HR compliance requirements.
intro.timingDesc: 10 questions across key MOM compliance areas
intro.honestDesc: Your score is only as accurate as your answers. When in doubt, choose "Not Sure".
intro.resultsDesc: Your compliance score, risk level, and recommended actions will appear immediately.

questionsPage.heading: HR Compliance Questions
questionsPage.analysingDesc: AIHR is now analysing your responses based on key Singapore HR compliance risk indicators.
questionsPage.disclaimer: This assessment is based on your responses and provides guidance only — not legal advice.
questionsPage.momAlert: Multiple WFA policy gaps identified — these are mandatory once the Act is in force.
questionsPage.cpfAlert: Hiring and selection evidence is weak — fair-consideration audits will land here first.
questionsPage.tafepAlert: Grievance and anti-retaliation controls missing — highest-risk area for complaints.

LOW.label: WFA Ready
LOW.subtitle: Your fairness posture is solid.
LOW.description: Core policies, evidence, and grievance channels are in place. Maintain by reviewing annually and refreshing training.
recommendations: Schedule annual WFA policy review and management sign-off
Re-run this checklist after major hires or org changes
Document the next training cycle for fair employment practices

MEDIUM.label: Gaps Identified
MEDIUM.subtitle: Several WFA obligations are not yet evidenced.
MEDIUM.description: You have a foundation but missing policies, documentation, or training will hurt under audit. Close gaps in the next 60–90 days.
recommendations: Draft or update the missing policy documents
Document interview standards, appraisal rubrics, and grievance procedures
Roll out fair-employment training and capture attendance
Engage HCCS for a structured WFA readiness review

HIGH.label: Significant Exposure
HIGH.subtitle: Foundational WFA controls are absent.
HIGH.description: Your organisation is exposed under the Workplace Fairness Act. Prioritise putting policies, channels, and evidence in place before any audit, complaint, or transition.
recommendations: Implement Workplace Fairness Policy with management endorsement immediately
Establish an anti-harassment policy and grievance procedure with documented channels
Train all staff and managers; capture attendance for evidence
Engage HCCS for end-to-end remediation and audit-ready documentation

questions.question: Does the Company has a Workplace Fairness Policy
Has senior management approved the policy?
Is the policy communicated to all employees?
Are job advertisements free from discriminatory wording?
Are interview questions standardized?
Are hiring decisions documented objectively?
Are compensation practices applied consistently?
Are appraisal criteria objective and measurable?
Are promotion opportunities communicated fairly?
Is there an anti-harassment policy?
Is there a Grievance Handling Policy in place
Are employees informed how to raise workplace fairness concerns?
Do employees receive workplace fairness training?
Does the company prohibit retaliation against complainants?
Are promotion trends analyzed for fairness?
Are recruitment agencies required to follow fair employment practices?
Are older workers treated fairly in hiring and promotion?
Are reasonable accommodations considered?
Are HR policies version-controlled?
Has the company performed a WFA readiness assessment?

faqs.q: Can I try before I subscribe?
faqs.a: Yes — our Free plan gives you access to basic AI HR chatbot queries, 3 templates, and a one-time compliance scan with no credit card required.

faqs.q: Is there a monthly billing option?
faqs.a: Currently all paid plans are billed annually. This helps us offer the best value and deliver consistent service to our members.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade my plan later?
faqs.a: Yes, you can upgrade at any time. Unused value from your current plan will be prorated toward your new plan.

faqs.q: Why is the Essential plan cheaper in Year 1?
faqs.a: We are currently running a 3-month launch promotional discount for early members. The promo price locks in for your first year.
Yes. All payments are processed securely via Airwallex. HCCS never stores your card details.

hrNews.title: HR News
hrNews.desc: Stay current with Singapore MOM, CPF, TAFEP, IRAS, and ACRA regulatory updates — curated for business owners and HR professionals.
hrNews.alertDesc: Get real-time alerts for MOM, CPF, IRAS, TAFEP, ACRA, and ICA regulatory changes relevant to your industry. Select your industry above to filter relevant news.
hrNews.subscribeNote: Subscribe to our newsletter for weekly HR regulatory updates delivered to your inbox.

newsItems.title: Flexible Work Arrangement Guidelines Now in Effect
newsItems.desc: The Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangements became legally enforceable. Employers must fairly consider all FWA requests in writing.

newsItems.title: Workplace Fairness Legislation — Key Changes for Employers
newsItems.desc: Singapore's Workplace Fairness Act strengthens protections against workplace discrimination. Employers must update policies by the effective date.

newsItems.title: Higher Monthly Salary Ceilings and Other CPF Changes in 2026
newsItems.desc: CPF monthly salary ceiling increases to S$8,000. Employers must update payroll systems to reflect new contribution rates.

newsItems.title: New MOM Guidelines on Retirement and Re-employment
newsItems.desc: Updated guidelines clarify employer obligations under the Retirement and Re-employment Act, including re-employment offers and retraining support.

newsItems.title: Salary Outlook 2026: Projected Increments Across Industries
newsItems.desc: MOM and industry surveys project average salary increments of 4–6% across most sectors, with Technology and Healthcare leading at 7–9%.

newsItems.title: Tech Pass Eligibility Criteria Updated for 2026
newsItems.desc: MOM has revised Tech Pass eligibility criteria to include product managers and AI specialists. Application windows open quarterly.

newsItems.title: Fair Hiring Framework — Audit Season Begins
newsItems.desc: TAFEP announces targeted compliance audits for companies with more than 25 employees. Fair consideration for locals must be documented.

newsItems.title: MOM Retrenchment Notification — Threshold Lowered
newsItems.desc: Companies retrenching 5 or more employees must notify MOM within 5 working days. Updated forms and filing procedures are now live.

hrNews.ctaTitle: Headline catching your eye? Find out if it affects your business.
hrNews.ctaBody: 30 minutes with the principal. We’ll tell you whether the regulation, ruling, or trend applies to you — and what to do about it.
hrNews.emptyBody: Curated MOM, CPF, TAFEP, and AGC updates will appear here as soon as they’re relevant to Singapore employers.
hrNews.detailNotFoundBody: This article may have been removed or is not yet published.

employmentLaws.title: Singapore Employment Laws
employmentLaws.desc: Key legislation every Singapore employer must know — from the Employment Act to the Workplace Fairness Act.
employmentLaws.ctaTitle: Need help ensuring your business is compliant?
employmentLaws.ctaDesc: Our MOM-registered HR consultants can audit your practices and guide you through every regulation.

laws.name: Employment Act
laws.desc: Core legislation covering employment contracts, working hours, overtime, leave entitlements, and termination for employees in Singapore.
keyPoints: Covers all employees except public servants, domestic workers, and seafarers
Mandates itemised payslips for all covered employees
Sets minimum annual leave, sick leave, maternity/paternity entitlements
Governs rest days, overtime rates, and salary payment timelines
Requires written employment contracts from Day 1

laws.name: Employment of Foreign Manpower Act
laws.desc: Regulates the employment of foreign workers and professionals in Singapore, governing work passes, employer obligations, and penalties.
keyPoints: Governs Employment Pass, S Pass, Work Permit, Tech Pass, and EntrePass
Mandates Fair Consideration Framework (FCF) job advertising requirements
Sets employer obligations for work pass holders' medical insurance
Penalties for illegal employment include fines and blacklisting
Employers must maintain valid work passes for all foreign employees

laws.name: Work Injury Compensation Act
laws.desc: Protects employees injured at work or who contract occupational diseases. Provides compensation without requiring proof of employer negligence.
keyPoints: Covers all employees except domestic workers and the self-employed
Mandatory Work Injury Compensation Insurance for manual and lower-paid workers
Employers must report work accidents to MOM within 10 days
Compensation for medical expenses, temporary incapacity, and permanent disability
Death compensation payable to dependants

laws.name: Workplace Fairness Act
laws.desc: Strengthens protections against workplace discrimination. Employers must not discriminate based on protected characteristics including age, race, religion, gender, and disability.
keyPoints: Prohibits discrimination in hiring, retrenchment, and employment terms
Protected characteristics: age, race, religion, gender, nationality, disability, marital status
Requires employers to have formal grievance handling processes
Employees can file complaints with MOM or TAFEP
Retaliation against complainants is prohibited

laws.name: Central Provident Fund Act
laws.desc: Mandates social security contributions for Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents. Governs contribution rates, account types, and employer obligations.
keyPoints: Applies to all SC and PR employees regardless of salary
2026 ordinary wage ceiling: S$8,000/month
Employer contribution rates vary by employee age bracket
CPF must be paid by 14th of the following month
Late contributions attract penalties and interest

laws.name: TAFEP Guidelines
laws.desc: The Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices sets guidelines for fair hiring, workplace harassment, and inclusive workplace practices.
keyPoints: Fair Consideration Framework (FCF) — job ads must be on MyCareersFuture for ≥28 days
Job criteria must be based on genuine requirements, not nationality or demographics
Employers must keep records of hiring decisions for 2 years
Sexual harassment policies must be in place for all employers
TAFEP audits target companies with high foreign employee ratios

laws.name: Fair Hiring Framework
laws.desc: A set of principles and practices to ensure merit-based, fair, and progressive hiring across Singapore's workforce.
keyPoints: All job postings must comply with TAFEP's Tripartite Guidelines
Selection criteria must be job-relevant and non-discriminatory
Employers must document and be able to justify all hiring decisions
Covers recruitment, promotion, and retrenchment decisions
Non-compliance can result in work pass privileges being curtailed

sections.heading: 1. Information We Collect
sections.body: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as your name, email address, phone number, company name, and any messages submitted via our contact or consultation forms. We may also collect usage data and analytics when you visit our website.

sections.heading: 2. How We Use Your Information
sections.body: We use the information we collect to respond to your enquiries, provide our HR consulting and advisory services, process consultation bookings, send relevant service information, and improve our website and services.

sections.heading: 3. Data Sharing
sections.body: We do not sell or rent your personal data to third parties. We may share information with trusted service providers (such as email and CRM tools) who assist us in operating our website and delivering our services, subject to strict confidentiality obligations.

sections.heading: 4. Data Retention
sections.body: We retain your personal data for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes outlined in this policy, or as required by law. You may request deletion of your data at any time by contacting us at enquiry@hccs.sg.

sections.heading: 5. Security
sections.body: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure. Our website uses HTTPS encryption and our databases are secured with access controls.

sections.heading: 6. Cookies
sections.body: Our website uses cookies and similar tracking technologies to improve user experience and analyse site traffic. You may disable cookies in your browser settings, though this may affect some site functionality.

sections.heading: 7. Your Rights
sections.body: Under Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA), you have the right to access, correct, and withdraw consent for the use of your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact us at enquiry@hccs.sg.

sections.heading: 8. Contact Us
sections.body: For any privacy-related queries or requests, please contact Human Capital Consulting & Services (Spore) Pte Ltd at enquiry@hccs.sg or via our contact form.

sections.heading: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.body: By accessing or using the HCCS website and services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.

sections.heading: 2. Services
sections.body: HCCS provides HR consulting, immigration advisory, compliance scanning tools, and AI-powered HR resources. Our services are provided on a professional advisory basis and do not constitute legal advice. For legal matters, please consult a qualified Singapore advocate and solicitor.

sections.heading: 3. Intellectual Property
sections.body: All content on this website, including text, templates, tools, and branding, is the property of Human Capital Consulting & Services (Spore) Pte Ltd. Reproduction or redistribution without written consent is prohibited.

sections.heading: 4. Membership & Payment
sections.body: Membership plans are billed annually. All payments are processed securely via Stripe. Refunds are evaluated on a case-by-case basis. By subscribing, you authorise HCCS to charge your designated payment method for the applicable subscription fee.

sections.heading: 5. Disclaimer of Warranties
sections.body: Our services and website content are provided "as is" without warranty of any kind, express or implied. HCCS makes no representations regarding the accuracy, completeness, or suitability of information for any particular purpose.

sections.heading: 6. Limitation of Liability
sections.body: To the maximum extent permitted by Singapore law, HCCS shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising out of or in connection with your use of our services, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.

sections.heading: 7. Governing Law
sections.body: These Terms of Service are governed by the laws of the Republic of Singapore. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Singapore courts.

sections.heading: 8. Contact
sections.body: For any queries regarding these terms, contact enquiry@hccs.sg.

about.heroDesc: Strategically building your Singapore workforce with expertise and integrity.

visionMissionBelief.title: Our Vision
visionMissionBelief.description: To be a trusted partner and steady backbone for business owners and global talent — delivering people-first, compliant, and forward-looking HR solutions.

visionMissionBelief.title: Our Mission
visionMissionBelief.description: We support entrepreneurs, SMEs, multinational companies, and foreign professionals through every stage of their journey — from business setup and immigration to HR management, statutory compliance, and AI-enabled HR solutions.

visionMissionBelief.title: Our Belief
visionMissionBelief.description: "We are not just here to process documents or manage compliance. We exist to carry the weight behind the scenes — so our clients can move forward 安心、踏实、从心出发。"

about.principalBio: IHRP-CP certified, ACTA-certified trainer with over 25 years of experience. Ker Bee Bee has secured over $5M in government training grants and is a regular speaker on the Employment Act and HR compliance. She serves on company boards and is deeply committed to people-first HR leadership.
about.principalQuote: “We provide the reliable backbone so you can move forward with confidence.”
about.ctaSubheading: Book a free 30-minute consultation to discuss your HR and workforce needs.

values.title: People-First Mindset
values.description: We believe effective HR begins with understanding people. We practise emotional intelligence, active listening, and empathetic communication to support our clients through both opportunities and challenges — especially in moments of pressure, change, and uncertainty.

values.title: Compliance Excellence
values.description: Compliance is the foundation of sustainable business. We apply strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and deep knowledge of HR regulations and employment laws to ensure our clients operate confidently, ethically, and in full alignment with statutory requirements.

values.title: Innovative Problem-Solving
values.description: We look beyond standard solutions to address real business challenges. Through critical thinking, creativity, and adaptive decision-making, we help clients find practical, efficient, and future-ready approaches — including the use of AIHR — to reduce complexity and improve outcomes.

values.title: Collaborative Partnerships
values.description: We work alongside our clients as trusted partners, not just service providers. By building strong relationships and practising open communication, negotiation, and teamwork, we co-create solutions and resolve issues constructively — even in complex or sensitive situations.

values.title: Continuous Learning
values.description: We believe growth begins with learning. We remain self-driven and reflective, continuously updating our knowledge, skills, and perspectives to stay relevant in an evolving regulatory, business, and technology landscape.

values.title: Client-Centric Service
values.description: Everything we do starts with our clients' needs. We listen carefully, respond promptly, and focus on practical solutions — delivering not just services, but reassurance, reliability, and measurable results.

advisors.name: Jennifer Yang
advisors.role: Lead Consultant | Strategic HR & People Systems

florence.title: About Ker Bee Bee
florence.role: Strategic HR Executive & Trusted People Advisor
florence.intro: Ker Bee Bee is a strategic, results-oriented HR executive and trusted people advisor with over 25 years of leadership experience across listed companies, multinational corporations, and SMEs, spanning energy, oil and gas, manufacturing, engineering, and F&B industries.

sections.heading: Core Competencies
items: End-to-end HR leadership across talent acquisition, C&B, L&D, policy, organisation development, and HRBP functions.
Change management, digital transformation, and HR due diligence for M&A and restructuring.
Regional ASEAN workforce leadership and cross-cultural people management.
Board-level HR initiatives, IPO readiness, and remuneration committee support.

sections.heading: Professional Qualifications
items: MSc in Organisational Development & HRM (Baruch College, City University of New York).
Bachelor of Business Administration (University of South Australia).
ACTA Trainer; Prosci Certified Change Management Professional; IHRP-CP.
Certifications in HR agility, data analytics, internal auditing, and transformation leadership.

sections.heading: Key Achievements
items: Secured over $5M in government training grants for companies.
Winner of the MOM Tripartite Standards Award for HR Excellence (2017).
Supported IPO readiness and strategic remuneration committee advisory work.
Led regional and global expansion projects with major cost savings outcomes.

jennifer.title: Ms Jennifer Yang
jennifer.role: Lead Consultant | Strategic HR & People Systems
jennifer.intro: Jennifer Yang is a business-minded HR leader with over 20 years of experience in bridging commercial strategy and people systems.

sections.heading: Professional Background
items: Over 20 years of experience in regional HR leadership and strategic consulting.
Expert in scalable people systems for SMEs and multinational corporations.
Strong track record in organisational transformation and cultural integration.
Specialist in workforce planning and performance management frameworks.

sections.heading: Areas of Expertise
items: Strategic HR Leadership
People Systems & Infrastructure
Organisational Scaling & Development
Cross-cultural Collaboration & Management
Workforce Transformation

loh.title: Mr Loh Hoon Sun (骆云山)
loh.role: Senior Strategic Advisor | Capital Markets & Wealth Structuring
loh.intro: Loh Hoon Sun is a highly regarded senior advisor in capital markets and wealth structuring with over five decades of cross-border experience across Singapore, Australia, and Malaysia.

sections.heading: Professional Background
items: Senior Advisor and former Managing Director at Phillip Securities.
Former EVP and Head of Treasury & Investment Banking at OUB.
Served on boards including SGX, SIMEX, and ACRA.
First Class Honours in Economics from The University of Queensland.

sections.heading: Areas of Expertise
items: Banking & Securities
Asset Management & Wealth Structuring
Real Estate & Resource-based Industries
Governance & Capital Preservation

sections.heading: Education & Credentials
items: FCPA Australia
CA Malaysia
FCA Singapore
FSID

ung.title: Mr Ung Gim Sei
ung.intro: Mr Ung Gim Sei focuses on intellectual property matters, including trademark applications and internet/media law, with 27 years of media industry leadership before legal practice.

sections.heading: Professional Background
items: Admitted to legal practice in 2003 after a 27-year media career.
Former Managing Director of Nanyang Siang Pau and Group General Manager at SPH.
General Manager of Shen Sin Daily with a special China circulation license.
Legal advisor to multiple associations and organisations.

sections.heading: Areas of Expertise
items: Intellectual Property & Trademarks
Internet & Media Law
Cyber Defamation
Chinese & Comparative Laws

sections.heading: Education & Honours
items: LL.M. (Distinction) in Chinese and Comparative Laws, City University of Hong Kong.
Graduate Diploma in Singapore Law, NUS.
PBM award recipient for cultural promotion.

james.title: Mr James Lee Ah Fong
james.intro: Mr James Lee is a senior lawyer with decades of active legal practice in Singapore across criminal, civil, and family law matters.

sections.heading: Professional Background
items: Partner at Ng, Lee & Partners and admitted to the Singapore Bar in 1981.
Former Assistant Superintendent of Police with FBI National Academy training.
Former President of Singapore Hainan Hwee Kuan.
Independent Director for listed companies including Cortina Holdings, TEE International, and TA Corporation.

sections.heading: Areas of Expertise
items: Criminal & Civil Litigation
Family Law
General Solicitor Work
Corporate Governance & Directorship

sections.heading: Credentials
items: Barrister-at-Law (Middle Temple)

complianceScanV1.title: HR Compliance Risk Scan
complianceScanV1.desc: Find out if your company is exposed to MOM regulatory risk. Answer 10 quick questions and receive an instant compliance score, risk rating, and recommended corrective actions — at no cost.
complianceScanV1.takesNote: Takes about 3 minutes · No account required

receiveItems.title: Compliance Score
receiveItems.desc: An instant percentage score across 10 key HR compliance areas.

receiveItems.title: Risk Level Rating
receiveItems.desc: Categorised as Low, Medium, High, or Critical risk with a plain-English explanation.

receiveItems.title: Action Checklist
receiveItems.desc: Specific corrective steps tailored to the gaps identified in your answers.

complianceScanV1.ctaTitle: Ready to check your compliance status?
complianceScanV1.ctaDesc: Free, instant, and no account required.

companyDetails.heading: Your Details
companyDetails.subheading: We'll use this to personalise your compliance report.

intro.aboutSuffix: against Singapore's key HR compliance requirements.
intro.timingDesc: 10 questions across key MOM compliance areas
intro.honestDesc: Your score is only as accurate as your answers. When in doubt, choose "Not Sure".
intro.resultsDesc: Your compliance score, risk level, and recommended actions will appear immediately.

questionsPage.heading: HR Compliance Questions
questionsPage.analysingDesc: AIHR is now analysing your responses based on key Singapore HR compliance risk indicators.
questionsPage.disclaimer: This assessment is based on your responses and provides guidance only — not legal advice.
questionsPage.momAlert: MOM Risk Alert: Possible gaps in contracts, payslips, records or termination process.
questionsPage.cpfAlert: CPF Risk Alert: CPF process may lack verification or control.
questionsPage.tafepAlert: TAFEP Risk Alert: Recruitment practices may not align with fair employment principles.

LOW.label: Low Risk
LOW.subtitle: Your company has solid HR compliance foundations
LOW.description: Your responses indicate a generally sound HR compliance structure. Continue periodic reviews to stay aligned with evolving regulations.

MEDIUM.label: Medium Risk
MEDIUM.subtitle: Several compliance areas need attention
MEDIUM.description: While no immediate critical issues detected, addressing identified gaps will reduce regulatory and operational risk.

HIGH.label: High Risk
HIGH.subtitle: Critical compliance gaps require immediate attention
HIGH.description: Your company has critical HR compliance areas that need urgent review. Taking immediate action will significantly reduce regulatory exposure.

questions.question: Do you currently employ foreign workers such as EP, S Pass or Work Permit holders?
Are CPF contributions calculated and submitted with proper checks in place?
options: Not sure or fully outsourced without checks

questions.question: Do all employees have updated employment contracts aligned with MOM Key Employment Terms?
Do you issue itemised payslips in line with MOM requirements?
Are your foreign employees' job roles, salaries and responsibilities aligned with MOM expectations?
Do you maintain proper HR records such as leave, attendance, contracts and warning letters?
Do you have a clear process for employee termination and dispute handling?
Does your company follow fair hiring practices and avoid potentially discriminatory recruitment language?
Do you actively monitor updates from MOM, CPF and TAFEP?
Do you have dedicated HR expertise, either internal or external, supporting compliance matters?
Does the Company has a Workplace Fairness Policy
Has senior management approved the policy?
Is the policy communicated to all employees?
Are job advertisements free from discriminatory wording?
Are interview questions standardized?
Are hiring decisions documented objectively?
Are compensation practices applied consistently?
Are appraisal criteria objective and measurable?
Are promotion opportunities communicated fairly?
Is there an anti-harassment policy?
Is there a Grievance Handling Policy in place
Are employees informed how to raise workplace fairness concerns?
Do employees receive workplace fairness training?
Does the company prohibit retaliation against complainants?
Are promotion trends analyzed for fairness?
Are recruitment agencies required to follow fair employment practices?
Are older workers treated fairly in hiring and promotion?
Are reasonable accommodations considered?
Are HR policies version-controlled?
Has the company performed a WFA readiness assessment?
